LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANT HEBREW HOME FOR THE AGED DISABLED'S MOTION TO STRIKE THE PUNITIVE DAMAGES ALLEGATIONS IN THE COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFF NATASHA KHAZANOV IS DENIED. THE MOTION PAPERS STATE THAT THE MOTION APPLIES ONLY IN THE EVENT THAT THE COURT SUSTAINED THE DEMURRER TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR ELDER ABUSE AND NEGLECT. SINCE THE COURT HAS NOT DONE SO, THIS MOTION IS DENIED. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANT HEBREW HOME FOR THE AGED DISABLED'S DEMURRER TO THE COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFF NATASHA KHAZANOV IS OVERRULED AS TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR ELDER ABUSE AND NEGLECT AND THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR NEGLIGENCE. AS TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR ELDER ABUSE AND NEGLECT, MS. KHAZANOV HAS SUFFICIENTLY PLED A CAUSE OF ACTION FOR NEGLECT UNDER THE ELDER ABUSE ACT PER FENIMORE V. REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA (2016) 245 CAL. APP. 4TH 1339. WHILE THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION IS ENTIRELY DUPLICATIVE OF THE FOURTH AND FIFTH CAUSES OF ACTION FOR WRONGFUL DEATH AND SURVIVORSHIP IN THAT A CLAIM FOR NEGLIGENCE RESULTING IN THE INJURY TO DEATH OF A DECEDENT MUST BE BROUGHT EITHER AS A WRONGFUL DEATH CLAIM OR A SURVIVOR CLAIM, THERE IS NO POSSIBILITY OF CONFUSION BY SEPARATELY ALLEGING A CLAIM FOR NEGLIGENCE APART FROM CLAIMS FOR WRONGFUL DEATH AND SURVIVOR. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

Re-Notice Of Hearing On Motion And Motion To Compel Defendants Further Responses To Request For Production Of Documents; Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Jul-13-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Deena Zacharin, Esq. (415-362-9800) of Stebner and Associates for Plaintiff.
Tessa Lessner, Esq. (415-438-6681) for Defendant.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court informs parties the supplemental briefing was received late after the tentative ruling was posted. Plaintiff argues untimeliness, requests 15 and 30, documents received and sanctions. Defendant submits to production of documents which is not an issue and argues sanctions.

The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument vacates the tentative ruling and rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF NATASHA KHAZANOV'S Re-Noticed Motion To Compel Defendants Further Responses To Request For Production Of Documents is Denied as Moot and Request For Sanctions is Denied at this time. Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Suzanne R. Bolanos, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR 711, ckaren@sftc.org =(302/SRB)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-22-2017 Set For Trial On Sep-11-17 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiff'S Complaint
Off Calendar Dec-14-2016 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court confirmed its tentative ruling. Defendant Hebrew Home for the Aged Disabled's motion to strike the punitive damages allegations in the complaint filed by plaintiff Natasha Khazanov is denied. The motion papers state that the motion applies only in the event that the court sustained the demurrer to the first cause of action for elder abuse and neglect. Since the court has not done so, this motion is denied. Order signed.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-14-2016 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court confirmed its tentative ruling. Defendant Hebrew Home for the Aged Disabled's demurrer to the complaint filed by plaintiff Natasha Khazanov is overruled as to the first cause of action for elder abuse and neglect and the second cause of action for negligence. As to the first cause of action for elder abuse and neglect, Ms. Khazanov has sufficiently pled a cause of action for neglect under the Elder Abuse Act per Fenimore v. Regents of the University of California (2016) 245 Cal. App. 4th 1339. While the second cause of action is entirely duplicative of the fourth and fifth causes of action for wrongful death and survivorship in that a claim for negligence resulting in the injury to death of a decedent must be brought either as a wrongful death claim or a survivor claim, there is no possibility of confusion by separately alleging a claim for negligence apart from claims for wrongful death and survivor. Order signed.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)
